reasoning: To reach ≤ $52,000 by March 31, 2026, Bitcoin must drop roughly 23% from its current price of ~$67,500. A standard barrier touch probability using typical Bitcoin volatility (around 50-65% annualized) for a 30-day horizon implies an 11-19% probability. Historical support around the $60,000 mark has held firm twice over the last month (early February and late February), which slightly suppresses the downside tail risk, although the broader short-term trend in 2026 has been bearish ('Crypto winter' drawdown from local Q4 2025 highs >$100k). Manifold traded at 15% 10 days ago (Feb 19), which roughly tracks the purely option-implied log-normal probabilities. Thus, an 18% probability encapsulates recent volatility, the distance to the strike, and the strong psychological and technical barrier at $60k that must first be breached.
